warnings: Unit "Unit-II Current Electricity" in Class XII is duplicated as a chapter name.
Unit "Unit–V Electromagnetic Waves" in Class XII is duplicated as a chapter name.
Unit "Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter" in Class XII is duplicated as a chapter name.
curriculum.subject: Physics (Theory)
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 14 chapters, and 3 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify and describe electric charges and fields
Evaluate electrostatic potential in various configurations
Explain the flow of electric current
Apply Ohm's law to circuit analysis
Determine magnetic effects of moving charges
Classify magnetic materials based on properties

topics: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits

practicals.title: To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current
practicals.unit_name: Unit–I Electrostatics
practicals.details: Determine resistance per cm using V-I graph

practicals.title: To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law
practicals.unit_name: Unit-II Current Electricity
practicals.details: Verify series and parallel resistance laws

practicals.title: To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge
practicals.unit_name: Unit-II Current Electricity
practicals.details: Find resistance using meter bridge

practicals.title: To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method
practicals.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Determine galvanometer resistance via half deflection

practicals.title: To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Identify electronic components

practicals.title: To observe the difference between a convex lens and a concave lens, and a convex mirror and a concave mirror; estimate power difference
practicals.unit_name: Unit–VI Optics
practicals.details: Observe lens/mirror differences and estimate power

practicals.title: To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of change in number of turns and introduction of ferromagnetic material on inductance
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design coil and analyze inductance factors

practicals.title: To design a step up and step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between input and output voltages
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design transformer and analyze voltage relations

marks_distribution: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism: 7 Marks
Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents: 4 Marks
Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter: 5 Marks
Unit–VIII Atoms and Nuclei: 12 Marks (Total for Unit)
question_paper_design: Structure: Theory Paper based on prescribed units and chapters
practical_assessment: Items for Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (All experiments)
List of Practicals: 1. Determine resistance per cm of wire by V-I graph
2. Verify laws of combination of resistances by Ohm's law
3. Find resistance using meter bridge
4. Determine resistance of galvanometer by half deflection method
5. Identify resistor, capacitor, inductor, diode from mixed collection
6. Observe difference between convex/concave lenses and mirrors; estimate power difference
7. Design inductor coil and know effect of turns/core material on inductance
8. Design step up/step down transformer and know input-output voltage relation

practicals.title: To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current
practicals.unit_name: Unit–I Electrostatics
practicals.details: Determine resistance per cm using V-I graph

practicals.title: To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law
practicals.unit_name: Unit-II Current Electricity
practicals.details: Verify series and parallel resistance laws

practicals.title: To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge
practicals.unit_name: Unit-II Current Electricity
practicals.details: Find resistance using meter bridge

practicals.title: To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method
practicals.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
practicals.details: Determine galvanometer resistance via half deflection

practicals.title: To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Identify electronic components

practicals.title: To observe the difference between a convex lens and a concave lens, and a convex mirror and a concave mirror; estimate power difference
practicals.unit_name: Unit–VI Optics
practicals.details: Observe lens/mirror differences and estimate power

practicals.title: To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of change in number of turns and introduction of ferromagnetic material on inductance
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design coil and analyze inductance factors

practicals.title: To design a step up and step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between input and output voltages
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents
practicals.details: Design transformer and analyze voltage relations

marks_distribution: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism: 7 Marks
Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents: 4 Marks
Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter: 5 Marks
Unit–VIII Atoms and Nuclei: 12 Marks (Total for Unit)
question_paper_design: Structure: Theory Paper based on prescribed units and chapters
practical_assessment: Items for Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (All experiments)
List of Practicals: 1. Determine resistance per cm of wire by V-I graph
2. Verify laws of combination of resistances by Ohm's law
3. Find resistance using meter bridge
4. Determine resistance of galvanometer by half deflection method
5. Identify resistor, capacitor, inductor, diode from mixed collection
6. Observe difference between convex/concave lenses and mirrors; estimate power difference
7. Design inductor coil and know effect of turns/core material on inductance
8. Design step up/step down transformer and know input-output voltage relation

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics and Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Understanding electric charges, fields, potential, and capacitance.
competencies: Identify and describe electric charges and fields

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Application of electrostatic potential concepts and capacitor systems.
competencies: Evaluate electrostatic potential in various configurations

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity
competency_groups.description: Fundamentals of electric current, resistance, and Ohm's law.
competencies: Explain the flow of electric current
Apply Ohm's law to circuit analysis

competency_groups.title: Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism
competency_groups.description: Interaction between moving charges, currents, and magnetic fields.
competencies: Determine magnetic effects of moving charges
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism and Matter
competency_groups.description: Properties of magnetic materials and their behavior in fields.
competencies: Classify magnetic materials based on properties
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction and AC
competency_groups.description: Induction phenomena and alternating current circuits.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current Systems
competency_groups.description: Specific applications and characteristics of AC circuits.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves
competency_groups.description: Generation, propagation, and properties of electromagnetic waves.
competencies: Describe the nature of electromagnetic waves

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
competency_groups.description: Reflection, refraction, and optical instrument design.
competencies: Apply laws of reflection and refraction

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics
competency_groups.description: Interference, diffraction, and polarization of light.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

marks_distribution: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism: 7 Marks
Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents: 4 Marks
Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter: 5 Marks
Unit–VIII Atoms and Nuclei: 12 Marks (Total for Unit)
question_paper_design: Structure: Theory Paper based on prescribed units and chapters
practical_assessment: Items for Identification/Familiarity with apparatus (All experiments)
List of Practicals: 1. Determine resistance per cm of wire by V-I graph
2. Verify laws of combination of resistances by Ohm's law
3. Find resistance using meter bridge
4. Determine resistance of galvanometer by half deflection method
5. Identify resistor, capacitor, inductor, diode from mixed collection

8. Design step up/step down transformer and know input-output voltage relation

outcomes: Identify and describe electric charges and fields
Evaluate electrostatic potential in various configurations
Explain the flow of electric current
Apply Ohm's law to circuit analysis
